title: Pseudocladia lateriflora
intro: Pseudocladia lateriflora, commonly known as Pseudocladia, is a species of red algae that belongs to the family Corallinaceae. The health benefits of Pseudocladia include its potential to exhibit antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory activities, which may contribute to its traditional use in treating various health conditions such as skin infections and respiratory diseases. The bioactive constituents of Pseudocladia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Pseudocladia is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and capsules, to treat various health conditions, although its use as a medicinal agent requires further scientific evaluation.
taxonomy: Pseudocladia lateriflora belongs to the family Apocynaceae and is classified under the order Gentianales. This classification places it within the angiosperm group of flowering plants. Pseudocladia lateriflora is often found in coastal areas and sandy soils.
